_comment: Neutral template placeholders (the 37 AI-GENERATE block finds), lifted verbatim from the proven golden fixture. These are TEMPLATE CONSTANTS — identical for every practice. generate.py pairs each find with a deterministic resolver (or keeps it verbatim) to emit a per-practice generated.json. Do not edit by hand; re-derive from a proven fill if the template changes.

meta_description.find: <meta name="description" content="Private psychotherapy with {{owner_name}}, {{credential}}. Specializing in {{specialties}}. {{location}} office and telehealth across {{service_areas}}." />

hero_headline.find: A different kind of therapeutic work.

hero_sub.find: {{practice_model}} private psychotherapy with {{owner_name}}, {{credential}}. Evidence-based work for adults at a turning point.

          ['4','Step four','Replace with the fourth move of {{method_name}}.'],

        ].map((c,i)=>`

method_intro_heading.find: A simple framework for the hard moments.

method_intro_lede.find: An evidence-informed framework {{owner_name}} uses with clients for the moment most therapy doesn't reach.

who_heading.find: People at a turning point who are ready to move.

who_body.find: Most clients arrive having already tried hard. They want a clinician who meets that with structure, evidence, and a steady hand.

populations_chips.find: ${['Specialty one','Specialty two','Specialty three','Specialty four','Specialty five','Specialty six'].map(p=>`${p}`).join('')}

pull_quote.find: "The work isn't about becoming someone new. It's about getting the obstacles out of the way of who you already are."

about_headline.find: {{owner_name}} is a {{credential_full}} who does structured, evidence-based work with people at a turning point.

about_body.find:
{{owner_name}}, {{credential}}, has spent years in clinical settings where the work mattered too much to be vague.

{{owner_name}} founded {{business_name}} and now sees adults privately for {{specialties}}. Licensed to serve {{service_areas}}, with telehealth and an in-person office in {{location}}.

The approach is evidence-based and outcomes-tracked — {{modalities}} — but the soul of it is simpler: people who do the work deserve a clinician who will too.

about_note.find: "I take this work seriously, and I expect the people I work with to do the same. If you're ready for that, I'm a good clinician to do it with."

career_heading.find: A career arc that built this practice.

career_lede.find: Each chapter taught a different piece of what this practice now is.

${[
          ['Year – Year','Organization','Role','One-sentence description of the work and a concrete accomplishment.'],

          ['Year – Year','Organization','Role','One-sentence description of the work and a concrete accomplishment.'],

        ].map(t=>`

approach_intro.find: Every modality below is supported by peer-reviewed research and used in combination — not as a brand.

session_shape.find:
Sessions are {{session_length}}. The first few are assessment-heavy — history, goals, and a baseline. From there, sessions are structured but not rigid: a check-in, the agreed clinical work, a between-session practice, and a brief close.

Trauma-informed throughout. Pace is set by the client's nervous system, not the calendar.

method_headline.find: A framework for the hard moments.

method_intro.find: The framework {{owner_name}} uses with clients to interrupt automatic patterns and choose a response that fits who they actually are. Each step pairs the science with one practice you can run this week.

method_why.find: "The name is a reminder of the one principle that holds the whole thing together — and the part most people skip."

journey_headline.find: A structured journey for people ready to do real work.

journey_intro.find: A phased structure, paced to the client and anchored to measurable change. This is the shape of the work, so you know what you're signing up for.

journey_phases.find:
${[
          ['Phase 01','30','Days · 1–30','Foundation','Assessment, baseline measures, and stabilization of the most disruptive symptom. We build the working alliance and identify what we are tracking.'],
          ['Phase 02','30','Days · 31–60','Excavation','Pattern identification and values clarification. We name what has been running the show and find out what you actually want instead.'],
          ['Phase 03','30','Days · 61–90','Reconstruction','Behavioral experiments and skills practice. The pace picks up; this is where the change becomes visible to the people around you.'],
          ['Phase 04','30','Days · 91–120','Integration','Relapse prevention, autonomy, and decisions about what comes next. You leave with the structure you needed in the first place.']
        ].map(p=>`

journey_reciprocity.find:
You bring honesty, the willingness to practice between sessions, and the assumption that change is possible. I bring evidence-based methods, structured tracking, and the steadiness that comes with real clinical experience.

Most clients see meaningful change earlier than they expect. The change that holds — the kind that survives a hard week — is the work of the later phases.

  • Card on file, charged at session
  • {{cadence}} cadence preferred

fees_why.find: A short, honest explanation of why this practice bills the way it does — what it protects about the clinical work, and why a free consult is the best way to find out if it's the right fit for you.

writing_intro.find: Essays on clinical work, frameworks, and the unglamorous mechanics of change — written for the client who wants to understand what's actually happening in the room.

writing_tags.find: ${['All','Tag two','Tag three','Tag four','Tag five','Tag six'].map((t,i)=>`${t}`).join('')}

contact_intro.find: Send a short message and {{owner_first_name}} will reply {{response_time}} to schedule a free {{consult_length}} consult — for both of us to see if we're a fit.
